section.name: Community guidelines
section.p: BreakTest coordinates public development through GitHub issues and pull requests. Please keep discussions focused, respectful, and reproducible.

Respect the discussion type
There are generally two types of public project discussions.

ul.li: Issues for bugs, regressions, feature requests, and documentation feedback.
Pull requests for concrete changes that are ready for review.

section.p:
Use the right project.
BreakTest is independent from Apache JMeter. Use BreakTest issues for BreakTest bugs and features. If you are discussing upstream Apache JMeter itself, use the upstream Apache JMeter project channels.

Ask actionable questions.
Include the BreakTest version, Java version, operating system, a minimal JMX file when possible, and a clear description of expected and actual behavior. For compatibility issues, include the Apache JMeter version you compared against.

Give feedback when you get a good answer.
If a response helped you solve a problem, add the resolution to the issue or pull request. Future users will find that context.

Keep your email short and to the point; use a suitable subject line.
Long reports are fine when the detail is useful, but lead with the problem, the expected behavior, and a minimal reproduction. Keep unrelated questions in separate issues.

Start a new issue for a new topic
Do not append unrelated questions to an existing issue. New topics are easier to triage, search, and close correctly.

Please don't send attachments or include large chunks of code
Before attaching JMX, JTL, screenshots, logs, or HAR files, remove secrets, tokens, customer data, and private endpoints. If a file is too large, create a small reproduction instead.

Report security issues privately.
Do not open public issues or pull requests for undisclosed vulnerabilities. Use the security policy.

Do not duplicate reports.
If you have more information for an existing issue, add it there instead of creating a second copy.

section.name: Conclusion
section.p: Now that you have read the guidelines above, see the community page for the current BreakTest discussion channels.
